title: AuditEvent

principal.description: The Principal that performed the interaction.

onBehalfOf.description: If the principal acted on behalf of another party (e.g. an assistant acting as a member), that party.

who.description: The acting principal, and the party it acted on behalf of, if any.

what.description: Which v0 interaction this AuditEvent records.

scope.description: The generalized-ownership attachment point the interaction occurred within.

when.description: Timestamp the interaction occurred, as an ISO 8601 date-time. A UTC `Z` designator or a numeric zone offset (e.g. `+12:00`) is accepted, so audit records from any timezone verify against the published schema.

outcome.description: Whether the interaction succeeded or failed.

degraded.description: Whether this interaction completed in a degraded mode that lost continuity it would otherwise have carried — e.g. a `migrate` whose Stage 2 transcript fetch failed and which proceeded with an empty seed instead (docs/spec/interactions.md § Degrade-to-fresh-start on transcript-fetch failure). Optional and additive: omitted (never `false`) for interactions with no degraded mode, and for a migrate that completed a full transcript seed. Present and `true` only on a degraded migrate. Without this field, a degraded migrate and a normal one both emit `outcome: 'success'` and are indistinguishable in the audit trail — the defect this field exists to close (issue #12).

sessionId.description: Identifier of the Session involved, if any.

conversationId.description: Identifier of the Conversation involved, if any.

definitionId.description: Identifier of the AgentDefinition involved, if any.

credentialIds.description: Identifiers of the Credentials attached/used in this interaction, so the trail names which credential — not just that one was attached.

refs.description: References to the session, conversation, definition, and/or credentials involved.

description: The normative audit record emitted for every mutating interaction.

PrincipalKind.description: The kind of acting party: a human user, a machine-to-machine service, or an agent acting on its own.

kind.description: The kind of principal being referenced.

id.description: Identifier of the referenced Principal resource.

PrincipalRef.description: A pointer to a Principal by kind and id, without embedding its full claims-contract record.

level.description: Which level of the tenant/workspace/user/group/role taxonomy this attachment point is at.

id.description: Identifier of the scoped entity (the tenant id, workspace id, user id, group id, or role id).

Scope.description: A generalized-ownership attachment point: a taxonomy level plus the identifier at that level.
